title: Delete legacy orchestrator test file after CLI migration
description: Remove `tests/test_orchestrator.py` once all remaining CLI tests have been migrated and the scheduler refactor has already relocated non-CLI coverage. This bead is limited to final cleanup of the obsolete file.
acceptance_criteria: `tests/test_orchestrator.py` is deleted only after its CLI coverage has been fully migrated.
No intended CLI or scheduler test coverage is lost during deletion.
Repository references are consistent after the obsolete test file is removed.

handoff_summary.completed: Confirmed the current migration state: test_cli_bead.py, test_cli_run.py, test_cli_merge.py, and test_cli_telemetry.py were created by dependency beads. No test in test_orchestrator.py is duplicated in any of those files or any other existing test file.
handoff_summary.remaining: Migrate all 65 remaining unique tests from test_orchestrator.py to appropriate dedicated files before deletion. Grouped by area: (1) CLI commands — test_command_plan_*, test_command_summary_*, test_command_tui_*, test_build_parser_*, test_cli_handoff_*, test_cli_retry_*, test_cli_summary_* → new test_cli_plan.py, test_cli_summary.py, test_cli_tui.py; (2) TUI internals — test_tui_* → extend or replace test_tui.py; (3) Worker prompts — test_worker_prompt_*, test_build_planner_prompt_*, test_load_guardrail_template_*, test_review_and_tester_templates_*, test_render_context_snippets_* → new test_prompts.py; (4) Schema — test_agent_output_schema_*, test_planner_output_schema_* → new test_schemas.py; (5) Planner — test_planner_*, test_write_plan_* → new test_planner.py; (6) Graph — test_render_bead_graph_* → new test_graph.py; (7) Storage — test_allocate_bead_id_*, test_resolve_bead_id_*, test_list_beads_*, test_old_sequential_ids_* → extend test_scheduler_beads.py or new test_storage.py. After all migrations, delete test_orchestrator.py.
handoff_summary.risks: Deleting test_orchestrator.py prematurely would permanently lose 65 test cases with no recovery path. Block is the correct outcome.
handoff_summary.next_action: Create migration beads for each remaining test group in test_orchestrator.py before retrying this deletion bead.
handoff_summary.block_reason: tests/test_orchestrator.py contains 65 unique tests not migrated by dependency beads. Groups: CLI commands (test_command_plan/summary/tui, test_build_parser, test_cli_handoff/retry/summary), TUI internals (test_tui_*), worker prompts (test_worker_prompt_* and related), schema (test_agent_output_schema_*, test_planner_output_schema_*), planner (test_planner_*, test_write_plan_*), graph (test_render_bead_graph_*), storage (test_allocate/resolve_bead_id_*, test_list_beads_*, test_old_sequential_ids_*). Deletion now violates acceptance criterion 2: 'No intended CLI or scheduler test coverage is lost during deletion.'
handoff_summary.design_decisions: N/A — no implementation was performed; bead blocked after pre-flight analysis.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Migration of remaining test groups is a prerequisite for this bead. Suggested split: test_cli_plan.py, test_cli_summary.py, test_cli_tui.py, test_prompts.py, test_schemas.py, test_planner.py, test_graph.py, and extensions to test_scheduler_beads.py or a new test_storage.py.

block_reason: tests/test_orchestrator.py contains 65 unique tests not migrated by dependency beads. Groups: CLI commands (test_command_plan/summary/tui, test_build_parser, test_cli_handoff/retry/summary), TUI internals (test_tui_*), worker prompts (test_worker_prompt_* and related), schema (test_agent_output_schema_*, test_planner_output_schema_*), planner (test_planner_*, test_write_plan_*), graph (test_render_bead_graph_*), storage (test_allocate/resolve_bead_id_*, test_list_beads_*, test_old_sequential_ids_*). Deletion now violates acceptance criterion 2: 'No intended CLI or scheduler test coverage is lost during deletion.'

execution_history.summary: Cannot delete tests/test_orchestrator.py — 65 unique tests remain that have not been migrated to any other file. The dependency beads (B-15b8cb14, B-d5497009) only migrated bead, run, merge, and telemetry CLI tests. Deleting the file now would permanently lose coverage across CLI commands, TUI internals, planner, worker prompts, output schema, graph rendering, and storage/bead-ID operations.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
